stem: MDI sequence admin: Which statement is MOST correct?
choices: This is an expected benign finding that requires no monitoring.
Stop the medication as soon as symptoms improve.
Use the bronchodilator first, then the inhaled corticosteroid.
Double the next dose if one is missed.
answer: Use the bronchodilator first, then the inhaled corticosteroid.
rationale: Correct: Use the bronchodilator first, then the inhaled corticosteroid. Bronchodilator opens airways; ICS can cause oral candidiasis.

stem: MDI sequence admin: Which finding requires the nurse to take immediate action/notify the provider?
choices: Mild transient nausea after the first dose
Rinse mouth after inhaled corticosteroid to reduce thrush.
Mild dry mouth without other symptoms
Occasional mild headache
answer: Rinse mouth after inhaled corticosteroid to reduce thrush.
rationale: Correct: Rinse mouth after inhaled corticosteroid to reduce thrush. Bronchodilator opens airways; ICS can cause oral candidiasis.

stem: MDI sequence admin: Which discharge teaching points are appropriate? (Select all that apply.)
choices: Use a spacer if prescribed to improve delivery.
Wait about 1 minute between puffs of the same inhaler.
Rinse mouth after steroid inhaler use.
Exhale into the spacer before inhaling.
Stop controller inhaler once symptoms improve.
Double the dose if you miss a dose.
answer: Use a spacer if prescribed to improve delivery.
Wait about 1 minute between puffs of the same inhaler.
Rinse mouth after steroid inhaler use.
rationale: Select the high-yield safety/teaching points. Bronchodilator opens airways; ICS can cause oral candidiasis.
